commentaries.author: Ephrem The Syrian
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh When Elijah said, “I alone am left as a prophet to the Lord,” he was not speaking against the just, because they were no [longer] to be found. Rather he was denouncing sinners, because they had done away with [the just]. He did not wish it to be, therefore, that he alone be found just, and for this reason, he could not be found by them for three years, for he had discovered that they were not worthy of being visited by God.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
I. Hebrew repeats "I "to make the contrast more striking. Elias alone was present, in this assembly, as the prophet of the Lord. (Haydock)
He knew that Abdias had preserved 100: but they were concealed.
Men. The other 400 of Jezabel did not appear.
